entry.title: Re: Implicit commit
entry.content:
Hi Shizofrenik,

wenn the programm ends, than will produce an Implicit Commit on the Database. That means, you have not an explicit Commit with the Command "COMMIT WORK [AND WAIT]" executed (the addition in the brackets means a synchronous executet commit: the Programm waits on tha Database. Otherwise is a asynchronous Commit and the Programm ends, while the Database is on "working").

I can mention, that in your programm the event START-OF-SELECTION is missing 😉

Congrats,

Attila

entry.title: Re: No dedicated error message found (SWF_FLEX_WEBGUI010) in SAP GUI for workflow scenario WS0200047
entry.content:
You have to be more specific than "how this can be solved". I already described (briefly) what I did. Are you asking for more details about that, or are you asking how parameters can be passed so a message with parameters can be displayed?

The short answer to the latter is that I haven't found a way to do that, and I don't think it is possible - at least not as of the 2023 version of S/4HANA.

To give a bit more detail on the former, I didn't just create a new message, I created a new exception class which inherits from CX_RSM_AGT_DETN_BUSI_EXCEPTION and raise an exception of that type when agent determination fails. Sorry about not writing that last time, it's an important piece of the puzzle.

The new exception class is only used in the BAdI implementations. Root cause exception(s) raised by the business logic are caught, and an exception of the CX_RSM_AGT_DETN_BUSI_EXCEPTION subclass is raised with the caught exception passed to the PREVIOUS parameter.

I hope that helps you determine how to solve your issue.

entry.title: Re: Aquire to decomission process
entry.content:
If you're looking to build the acquisition process within S/4HANA PM, there is not best practice solution for it. You need to design the process according to your business needs.

That said, a high-level sketch of the process could begin with AUC creation, followed by the PR-PO cycle for procurement. The AUC is then settled against an asset for which equipment are created. Then you could create a WO to record their installation process. This could be an existing maintenance order or you could configure a new one just for this purpose. 

This is just a high-level idea. You may need to use internal orders, for capital expenditure part, you may also need to enable serialization if required. 

These are good resources: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/enterprise-asset-equipment-track-and-trace/ba-p/13473502

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/procurement-of-assets-under-construction/ba-p/13279679

entry.title: Re: Difference between Data Element and domain
entry.content:
Domain – It defines the technical attributes of a field, such as data type, length, and value range.

Data Element – It defines the semantic meaning and also includes the technical attributes of a field.

entry.title: Re: SAC – Integrated Financial Planning – DRIVER6 & alloc...
entry.content:
Dear @Aleque,

the End-to-End Planning Process is a sample story to explain how the different parts of the content integrate to each other and a sample planning process could look like. Therefore we deliver the business content with sample data to enable users to test the content without the need to connect to their own data.

If you conected your SAC tenant with your S/4HANA backend and imported your own data you also might want to import the drivers. To do this, please follow the instructions in the SAP Help portal, Importing Statistical Key Figures to SAP Analytics Cloud (SAP S/4HANA Cloud / SAP S/4HANA)
As an alternative you can manually plan the drivers using the SAP_FI_IFP_IM_OPEX_PlanAdmin (SAP S/4HANA Cloud / SAP S/4HANA) story.

Regarding your questions:

  1. You can either load the drivers from your S/4Hana backend or manually plan them in SAC.
  2. If you deleted the sample data in the OPEX model and imported your own fact data, you might have missed to upload the drivers, in this case they are null.
  3. There can be null values for drivers, even if the import of data was successful. This is depending on what data you're uploading.

I would recommend you our documentation Financial Planning in SAP Analytics Cloud - Getting Started with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAP S/4HANA Cloud / SAP S/4HANA)

Please let me know if this answers your questions.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Performing actions from Work Zone notifications and handling it in CAP application
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have followed the Developing Cloud Foundry Applications With Notifications tutorial and successfully created an application with notifications working as expected.

Now, I am trying to enhance it by adding actions to the notifications. I am currently facing an issue in the following scenario:

Use Case: Leave Request Action

  • I have a Leave Request entity.
  • When a leave request is created, a notification is sent to the approver.
  • The notification includes an action button – for example: "Reject Leave".
  • When the user clicks the Reject action, the backend CAP Java application receives a call to the /ExecuteAction API.

 

Problem:

In the /ExecuteAction call, I want to receive the Leave Request ID (or some unique record identifier) so that I can find and reject the correct record in the backend.

I tried adding a placeholder like {leaveRequestId} in the ActionId or TargetParameters, but this did not work.

What I Need Help With:

  • How can I pass the leave's record ID or other dynamic parameters to the backend via the /ExecuteAction payload?
  • Is there a supported way to include contextual data in the action definition or payload so that the backend knows which record to act on?

entry.title: Re: Issue creating storage bins via LS01N – Missing LGTYP...

Storage types and storage sections are defined in SPRO path: Logistics Execution -> Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Define Storage Type and Define Storage Sections.

These are basic LE-WM settings defining warehouse structure.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Users can confirm a Production Task even after changing input item quantity to 0, bypassing the BOM
entry.content: In SAP Business ByDesign (SAP ByD), during production confirmation, the system typically uses the Bill of Materials (BOM) assigned to the Product Model to determine the quantity of input components via backflush. If users are able to manually change the input component quantity (e.g., setting it to 0) and still confirm the production task, that is a process/control issue — especially if it should be restricted to ensure BOM accuracy and material traceability.

entry.title: SAP Build Appsの新機能：生成AIによるエンティティからのページ作成
entry.content:
SAP Build Appsに生成AIを使用した非常に便利な新機能が追加されましたので御紹介したいと思います。
BTP Destinationを使用して作成されたエンティティを利用してのそれの読み込み・作成・編集・削除用のページの自動作成機能です。非常に楽になりますよ。

前準備

まず、生成AI機能を有効にする必要があります。有効にするにはSAP Build Appsではなく、SAP Build LobbyからControl Tower→Generative AIをクリックし

Enable Generative AIのスイッチを有効にします。

これで本機能が使用できるようになります。

注意その１：
このスイッチを有効化することにより、同じサブアカウントにあるSAP Build Process AutomationのGenerative AI機能も有効化されます。SAP Build Appsだけ/SAP Build Process Automationだけ有効にするということは出来ませんのでご注意ください。双方とも現在開発中のプロジェクトに影響は有りませんが、開発ツールの中に"これまで表示されていなかったボタンが表示される"という形で機能が追加されますので必要そうであれば開発メンバーに事前にアナウンスしたほうが良いかもしれません。
注意その２：
現在SAP Build Apps/SAP Build Process Automationの生成AI機能には使用回数等制限が設けられています。今回紹介するSAP Build Appsのページ生成機能は「1ヶ月1ユーザーあたり100リクエスト」という制限があります。

 

 

使ってみる

この機能はBTP Destinationを使用したIntegrationに機能します。テストとしてはCUDの操作が確認できるようにCAPを使用してSAP HANA Cloudデータベースのテーブルに対してODataを使用できるようにしたものが望ましいですが、ない場合はおなじみのNorthwindサービスに対してDestinationを設定しても良いでしょう。

BTP DestinationをIntegrationsでSAP Build Appsで使用できるようにします。ODataであればその中のエンティティを有効化すると

このようにこれまでなかった「Generate Pages」というボタンが表示されます。早速クリックしてみましょう。

ページ一覧を確認すると

このようにページが3つ追加され、スタートページに設定したページに一覧表示へのリンクボタンが追加されます。プレビューで実行してテストしてみましょう。

スタートページに追加されたボタンをクリックすると

データの一覧表示のページに移行します。右上のAddボタンをクリックすると

データの追加用のフォームに移動します。記入してSaveボタンをクリックすると実際に新規行として保存することが出来ます。

一覧表示のページで表示されている行をクリックすると

その行のデータの詳細表示することが出来ます。右上のDeleteボタンをクリックすると

ダイアログが表示され、実際に削除することが出来ます。Editボタンをクリックすると

データ編集用のページに飛び、実際に編集保存することが可能です。

ほぼ一瞬でこのようにページが生成されます。大したものです。

私が個人的に「おっ！」と思ったのが、いま1つのエンティティに対してページを生成しました。この状態で他のエンティティに対してのページを生成させると

このようにスタートページにもう一つ追加したエンティティ用のボタンが表示されることです。メニューのようになりますので便利ですね。

生成されたページは当然編集が可能です。好きなデザインに変えるもよしですし、例えば削除は出来ないように削除用のロジックやボタンを削除するということも出来ます。現在は入力フォームなどでデータ型のチェックは行っておらず、例えば数値型のフィールドに文字を入力することが出来てしまう等の問題がありますので、加えるとしたらそういうロジックでしょうか？
突き詰めていくと不満は出てきますが、しかしながらここまで簡単にページが生成できるというのは非常に楽です。例えばデータベース上のマスターテーブルの編集アプリなんてODataで公開してさえいれば、この機能を使えばあっという間に出来てしまうということになりますので。

アプリのベースとして十分使える機能です。ぜひ使ってみてください。

※現在BTP DestinationのREST APIに関しては動作はするものの、listで返されるデータを基準にしてロジックを組んでしまうようで CUD操作に関してはうまく動きません。ODataのみ対応と考えたほうが良さそうです。

entry.title: Is it possible to change S-User first and last name?
entry.content:
Would it be possible to update the First and Last name for user?  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to change S-User first and last name?
entry.content:
According to KBA2597456 - How to update the S-user ID name - SAP for Me - SAP for Me , currently it is not possible to change the name on an S-user ID without contacting SAP. S-user IDs are intended to be used only by one individual since there is history tied to each S-user ID (e.g. authorizations, incidents, Service Messages, Certifications, etc). Due to this, SAP does not allow S-user IDs to be reused/transferred/overwritten to other individuals -- and the e-mail address should match the S-user's first and last name.

To prevent reuse of an S-user ID by a different person, it is currently not allowed to change personal contact details (first and last names) for an S-user ID. Note: S-users can update limited information for their own S-user ID such as phone number or e-mail address, and some data can only be changed by the administrators (see KBA 1271545).

If a name update is required for an S-user ID, SAP can make the changes only for the following reasons as long as the S-user ID is for the same person (not a different person):

  • Name is spelled incorrectly
  • First name and last name (surname) data is transposed in the wrong fields
  • The first letter is mistakenly lowercase.
  • Legal name changed (e.g. marital status change, gender change, etc.)

entry.title: SAP PS Today
entry.content:
SAP Project Systems(PS) today

 This Blog is the ready reckoner for the SAP PS as on date and the journey with SAP B1, ECC, S4 HANA, S4 HANA -Professional Services.

High Level Comparison

 

SAP B1

SAP ECC

S4 HANA (1909 & 2020)

S4HANA-Public Cloud (Grow with SAP)

S4 HANA- On Prem (RISE with SAP)

Use the Project Management module to manage your projects from start to finish, centralizing all project related transactions, documents, resources, and activities. The feature helps you to monitor the progress of tasks, stages, subprojects, analyze budget costs, and generate reports on various aspects of the project, such as stage analysis, open issues, and resources.

The Project System (PS) in SAP ECC 6.0 is a module that helps manage projects from start to finish. It allows users to monitor and control all aspects of a project, including technical and commercial aspects.

The PS (Project Systems) module in SAP S/4HANA is a project management tool that helps organizations plan, manage, and execute projects.

The SAP Project System (PS) module in SAP S/4 HANA is part of the GROW with SAP program, which helps companies grow their business by adopting SAP S/4 HANA Cloud. The PS module helps with project planning, execution, monitoring, and more. 

The Project System (PS) module in SAP S/4HANA on-premise helps project managers plan, execute, and close projects. It also helps analyze project data and integrate with other modules. 

 

How it works:

Project Type

BP Code

Contact Person

BP Territory

Sales Employee

Owner

Project with Subprojects

Project Name

Project Number

Status

Start Date

Closing Date

Due Date

Open activities

Completeness %

Financial Project

How it works:

  • Work breakdown structure (WBS)

Users can create a WBS to structure their project by phase, logic, or object. 

  • Material transfer

Users can transfer material components from the product structure of Integrated Product and Process Engineering (iPPE) to the PS. 

  • Project planning

Users can create detailed plans for the project, including individual activities and work packages. 

  • Project execution

Users can ensure that the project is executed efficiently, on time, and within budget. 

  • Project monitoring

Users can monitor the progress of the project, including technical and commercial aspects. 

 

How it works:

  • Project planning: Define project structures, work breakdown structures (WBS), and project networks 
  • Resource management: Allocate and manage resources like personnel, materials, and equipment 
  • Project scheduling: Create and manage project timelines, dependencies, and critical paths 
  • Budgeting and cost control: Create project budgets and track actual costs against planned budgets 
  • Integration: Integrates project data with other SAP modules like FI (Finance), CO (Controlling), MM (Materials Management), and HR (Human Resources)

 

How it works:

  • The PS module is integrated with other tools like MSP and Primavera
  • The PS module can help you quickly calculate product costs and the impact of changes

Who it's for 

  • GROW with SAP is designed for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and startups that are looking to grow
  • It's also a good option for companies that prefer a high degree of standardization and want to use the public cloud

 

How it works:

  • Set up master data: Define project system master data, including work breakdown structures (WBS) and networks
  • Plan and execute projects: Plan project costs and logistics, and execute project activities
  • Close projects: Manage period-end close for plan and actual data
  • Analyze project data: Use Project System reports to analyze project structures, financials, and logistics
  • Integrate with other modules: Integrate with other modules, such as portfolio and project management (PPM)

 

RISE with SAP 

  • RISE with SAP is a managed cloud service that helps organizations migrate from on-premise ERP software to the cloud
  • RISE with SAP includes cloud ERP, process intelligence, and execution, along with migration services

 

 

Usage of Project Systems:

Pharma,

Manufacturing,

Professional Services

Aerospace & Defense

Utilities

Real Estate Management

 

PS Integrations with other modules/packages:

MM-Materials Management

SD – Sales and Distribution

FICO- Financial Accounting & Controlling

RE-FX- Flexible Real Estate Management

SAP -Joint Venture Accounting

HCM – Human Capital Management

PM – Plant Maintenance

Concur – Travel & Expense

CATS – Cross Application Time sheet

EPPM – Enterprise Portfolio & Project Management

SAC – SAP Cloud Analytics

 

Use friendly accessories today for PS;

Fiori Apps

Bots

Automation

Testing Tools

Migration Tools

 

Conclusion:

SAP PS has evolved from ECC days to right now Professional S4 HANA – Public Cloud. Tailor made suits to fulfill different business segment demands. Implementation time has come down drastically from 12-16 months to 6-8 months. SAP PS is being used and adopted globally on a large scale by users than before and this module is here to stay.

entry.title: SAP HANA Meets Gen AI 🤖 | Build Your Own AI Assistant with GPT-4 + Streamlit !
entry.content:
The Starting Point

In this blog post, I'm sharing my dive into the world of Generative AI.

 Like many, I'm fascinated by what Large Language Models (LLMs) can do - writing code, drafting emails, even creating images. But on their own, they're often disconnected from the live data businesses run on. You can't just re-train them every day; it's too costly and slow. This got me thinking: how do we bridge that gap?

How can LLMs tap into real-time information or query our complex company databases, maybe even those with millions of records and intricate relationships?

This is where I started exploring Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and AI agents. RAG is great for feeding relevant context to the LLM, whether from documents or other sources. Agents can act on instructions, like querying databases or using APIs.

Agents represent systems that intelligently accomplish tasks, ranging from executing simple workflows to pursuing complex, open-ended objectives.

Building agents involves assembling components across several domains—such as models, tools, knowledge and memory, audio and speech, guardrails, and orchestration.

For the deeper technical aspects, this post assumes a basic understanding of common AI terms such as tokenization, vector embeddings, and prompting methods. However, the main architectural concepts will be explained simply

Why Gen AI Outperforms Traditional Programming?

  • Traditional: You usually need specific buttons, forms, dropdowns, or exact commands. If you want to ask for sales in a slightly different way, you often can't – the programmer had to anticipate exactly how you'd ask.
  • Gen AI: You can just talk or type naturally! Ask "Show top sellers," "Who sold the most?", or "What products are flying off the shelves?" – the AI is designed to understand the meaning behind your words, even if they're phrased differently each time. This makes it way more intuitive and less rigid.
  • Traditional: Code needs precise instructions. If you misspell "Eclairs" as "eclars," a traditional search might just fail unless the programmer specifically added code to handle common typos (which is hard to do exhaustively!)
  • Gen AI: It's much better at handling ambiguity, typos, or slightly unclear requests. It can often figure out you meant "Eclairs" or ask you a clarifying question if needed, rather than just giving up. It rolls with the punches better.
  • Traditional: If management suddenly wants a new type of report or chart, a developer usually needs to go in and write new, specific code for that exact report format or SQL query.
  • Gen AI: You can often just ask for the information in a new way. The LLM, guided by its instructions and tool descriptions, can potentially generate the new SQL or figure out how to combine existing tools differently on the fly, without needing explicit code changes for every single variation.
  • Traditional: Best suited for well-defined tasks where the steps are clear (e.g., "Show sales for this specific product ID").
  • Gen AI: Can attempt more exploratory questions like "Are there any interesting trends in the sales data for last quarter?" or "Summarize the performance in the UK." It can use its reasoning ability (and the tools you give it) to try and figure out how to approach these less rigidly defined problems.

Note: It's not magic! Gen AI needs clear instructions (your System Prompt), access to the right tools (your Agents), and sometimes makes mistakes (hallucinations or incorrect assumptions). As of now, Traditional programming is still essential for building the reliable tools the AI uses, handling complex business logic precisely, and ensuring security and data integrity.

The real power often comes from combining them – using Gen AI for the flexible, natural language understanding and planning front-end, and traditional programming for the robust, reliable back-end tools and agents that interact with databases and APIs

I wanted to create something where anyone could just ask questions about our sales data (stored in SAP HANA Cloud) in plain English and get answers, charts, or even related info without needing to know SQL or navigate complex dashboards.

Below is the Architectural Diagram for better understanding.

🌐Tech Stack:

💾SAP HANA Cloud

🧠 GPT-4o (OpenAI)

🛠️ Streamlit + Python

🧪 Google Colab + Ngrok

🗃️ Real sales data from Kaggle

Step 1: You Ask a Question

It all starts with you! You type your request into the chat interface of our Streamlit app – maybe something like "Show me sales for Product A" or "What's the weather in Berlin?". This is your Input Prompt.

Step 2: The App Sends Your Request to the Open AI LLM

The Streamlit app (our lightweight user interface) takes your question and sends it off, along with some context and built-in instructions, to the OpenAI LLM (Large Language Model - gpt-4o).

Step 3: The LLM Understands and Plans

This is where the AI magic happens! The LLM uses its powerful Natural Language Processing (NLP) capabilities to understand what you're asking for. It looks at your specific words ("sales", "Product A") and combines that with its knowledge and the specific instructions we gave it (the "System Prompt").

Based on this understanding, it figures out:

  • What needs to be done? (e.g., "I need to query the sales database.")
  • Which Agent is best for the job? (e.g., "Agent A, the database expert, should handle this.")
  • What specific information does that specialist need? (e.g., "I need to generate an SQL query to find 'Product A')

It then generates precise instructions, often including generated code like SQL, formatted in a JSON.

Step 4: The App Delegates to the Right Agent

The Streamlit app receives the JSON instructions back from the LLM. It reads the instruction, sees which agent the LLM selected (like Agent A), and what input it needs (like the SQL query). The app then calls the specific Agent function within our code.

Step 5: The Agent Gets to Work

The activated Agent takes the input provided by the app (as instructed by the LLM) and performs its specialized task:

  • Agent A (Database Agent): Connects to the database (SAP HANA or others) and runs the SQL query.
  • Agent B (API Agent): Calls an external API (like a weather service or internal company API).
  • Agent C (Web Search Agent): Performs a web search to find information.

The agent interacts with its designated backend system to fetch the required data or perform the action.

Step 6: Checking In & Getting Ready for the Next Step

Once the Agent finishes its task, it sends the result (like the data found, the weather report, or a success/error message) back to the Streamlit app.

The app then sends an Observation message back to the LLM. This is super important! It tells the LLM: "Okay, Agent A ran the query, and here's what happened (e.g., 'found 10 rows' or 'encountered an error')." This allows the LLM to:

  • Track the progress of your request.
  • Handle any errors gracefully.
  • Plan the next step if it's a multi-part task (like getting data then creating a chart).

Step 7: Delivering the Final Answer

Once the LLM determines the task is complete (either after one step or multiple steps), it generates the final, user-friendly response. It sends this final response (again, likely in that JSON format with "step": "output") back to the Streamlit app.

The app takes this final text and displays it back to you in the chat window.

And that's how your simple question gets turned into a specific action and a clear answer, all orchestrated by the AI brain and executed by specialized agents!

Demo :

To see how this works in practice, I loaded up some chocolate sales data from Kaggle into our HANA Cloud instance ( you can connect with any DB ) . I also connected to a free weather API and even made a simple agent using Python's OS tools to create files on your local desktop or if you run this in your vs code  , even can generate code files for your project based on instructions ( like mini cursor 😀 )

Here are some of the things I asked it:

Show me the top 3 best-selling products based on total sales amount and display the results clearly.

Result: It correctly figured out it needed to SUM the sales, GROUP by product, ORDER them, and LIMIT to 3. It generated the SQL and showed me the table!

You can even see behind the curtain in the "Execution Details" to see the SQL it wrote.

Can you show me sales of eclars  -  (Deliberate typo!)

  • Result: Success! Because we told the AI brain (in the System Prompt) to always use case-insensitive searching (UPPER()), it found the sales data for "Eclairs" even with my typo.

In Other Language : 

Muestra los tres productos más vendidos según el monto total de ventas y muestra los resultados claramente  -

Create a line chart comparing the monthly sales trend for our two key products: '85% Dark Bars' and '99% Dark & Pure'. Title it 'Monthly Sales Trend: Key Products'. – Complex Visualization

Result: This was more complex! It first ran a query to get the monthly sales for both products, then correctly called the charting agent to create a line chart with two distinct lines – perfect for visual comparison.

What was the exact total sales amount generated in the USA for August 2022 and weather of New York?

  • Result: This was cool – it seamlessly called the HANA agent for the sales figure and then called the Weather agent for NYC, presenting both pieces of information in the final answer. Two agents working together!

Negative Testing:

Show me all the data in the chocolate sales table.

Result:  The assistant blocked this! It recognized that asking for the entire table without filters is usually a bad idea. This is possible with System Prompt.

While this prototype provides a glimpse of the potential, the underlying agentic architecture is designed to be scalable and can be further developed using industry-standard practices for security, monitoring, and robustness.

So, what does this all mean? By blending the conversational Power of an LLM with focused agents that can securely talk to our real systems (like HANA, APIs, or other tools), we're building more than just a Q&A bot.

We're creating tools that feel natural, breaking down technical barriers and putting powerful data insights directly into more people's hands. It feels like we're just scratching the surface – I truly envision a future where this kind of seamless AI integration becomes the norm, making all our tools smarter, friendlier, and ultimately, more helpful.

Github link : 

https://github.com/abhimanyu-agi/genAI--SAP-HANA-AI-Assistant

Demo Video:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UApzc5pQLXI

entry.title: Re: SAP GRDC AD hoc items Transport
entry.content:
Hi Taliya,

The status quo is that ad hoc items cannot be transported.

If you transport forms that make use of ad hoc items, create ad hoc items with the same names on the target system using the Define Ad Hoc Items app. The newly created ad hoc items are automatically used in the respective forms based on their identical name.

To avoid losing the connection between ad hoc items and form definitions, complete the ad hoc item creation on the target system before you import form definitions to the same target system.

Kind regards,
Marcel

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting providing components to the vendor (SD...
entry.content:
You need to bifurcate the process into two
One for FERT material x, explore the Third Party functionality. That is once the sale order is saved, a PR would be generated subsequent to which, PO to generate for material X which will be supplied directly by the vendor. Once the material is supplied by vendor, invoice can be generated directly with the buyer and in the meanwhile, settle the vendor account accordingly.

Then, for components, create sub-contracting PO for X1 & X2 components for which, the vendor can be paid as service charges.

entry.title: Re: Dear Experts,We are using the same material codes for...

From  Maintain HSN/SAC Codes (online help)

  • Harmonized System Nomenclature (HSN) code is a product coding system to determine the correct applicable rate (including exemptions, if any) for goods
  • Service Accounting Code (SAC) is a set of codes that classify and categorize services

Could you elaborate on your scenario?

3月成本中心实际费用发生额为53000元，8362.99USD,但计算成本中心实际成本率时的单价无法得知金额是如何计算出来的

2、MM03物料主数据维护的结算规则为17:18=4:1，如图

修改工单结算规则为17:18=3:2

CKM3N:显示物料17，如下：

CKM3N:显示物料18，如下：

entry.title: Re: Big memory consumption XSLT, why Heap is used? Best with ST.
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

> I'd like to understand why Heap is used although EM is only few hundreds of MB and no other task in the system.
can you post the output of RS MEMORY? HEAP Memory for DIALOG processes is used when EM Quota is reached. it is workprocess local memory an not released until the wp is restarted (this is why we call it priv). Measure memory Usage in SAT with not aggregated trace should show you peak memory usage i think.

Kind regards,

Hermann

entry.title: Get Certified: Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Please ask a question in the Q&A area
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 


In this Get Certified live session series, certified expert trainers will deliver knowledge transfer sessions covering all topic areas relevant for the C_S4CPB certification: SAP Certified Associate – Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

The goal of this Get Certified live session series is for learners to receive expert-guided support to complement their self-study activities and prepare them to attempt the C_S4CPB certification exam.

To prepare for the certification, you should first work through the Learning Journey (s) linked to the certification, and join the live sessions. Your trainer will review the Learning Journey content and focus on the most important topics relevant to the exam, to give you the best chance of success.

The Kick Off Session (Session 1) and the Wrap Up Session (Session 18) are included in the booking. Please book sessions 2 - 17 individually using the links provided below. You can book all sessions or just individual sessions to fill existing knowledge gaps.

  • Session 1: Kick Off: Outlining the live session series, introducing the certification, explaining the types of questions, providing tips for preparation, working through sample questions in the exam interface. (1 hour)
  • Session 2: Foundational Concepts of Cloud Software and SAP Business Suite
  • Session 3: Differentiating GROW and RISE with SAP for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Session 4: Implementing with a Cloud Mindset for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public and Private Edition
  • Session 5: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Activities in a Nutshell
  • Session 6: Building the Implementation Team & Managing Change during SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects
  • Session 7: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 3-System Landscape: Provisioning, Transports, & Release Upgrade
  • Session 8: Setting Up the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Project in SAP Cloud ALM
  • Session 9: Using SAP Central Business Configuration to Activate Content in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Starter System
  • Session 10: Preparing for Fit-to-Standard Workshops in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementations
  • Session 11: Conducting Fit-to-Standard Workshops in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementations
  • Session 12: Using SAP Central Business Configuration to Activate Content in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Development System
  • Session 13: Maintaining and Customizing Business Roles, Spaces, and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Session 14: Customizing Fiori Applications with In-App Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Session 15: Setting Up Integrations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Session 16: Migrating Data with the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Session 17: Using the Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Session 18: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Certification Wrap-Up

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release Highlights on SuccessFactors Learning Management System
entry.content:
This article highlights few latest upgrade & enhancements designed in SuccessFactors Learning to provide seamless and efficient learning experience for end users and admins.

1. Banners can be Paused now :

If multiple banners are being used by a customer, users can pause whichever banner they want . A new Pause/Play Button has been added(as shown below) :

2. AI generated Image in Learning :

Now we can generate image using AI while creating or editing custom cards and banners, and when modifying existing items, programs, and curricula. Once we select 'Generate image Using AI' a text box widget will appear where we can give the description of the image and in minimum 15 seconds AI will Generate the image.

                                                                   Source: SAP

Configuration Requirement : 

* Customer must have AI units license.

* Customer enabled the AI Services Administration  > Assisted Image Generation setting in Admin Center.

3. Verified Email Domain Synchronization : Automated

Earlier domain validation ran daily as a default schedule. However, now an automatic process job available for this process. This automatic job now synchronizes the latest list of verified email domains from the SAP SuccessFactors Platform to Learning . Automatic Sync for Verified Email Domains is available on System Administration >Automatic Processes > Verified Email Domain Synchronization , Admin can control the frequency (Daily,Monthly). If any discrepancy found logs can be checked on 'view Error' button.

 

                                                                               Source: SAP

4. Supervisor's control on Overdue Training Reminders

An impactful feature where supervisor can send overdue courses reminder mail for his/her subordinates . The control of reminder mail's context like Subject line and body of the mail is editable by Supervisor.

                                                                        Source: SAP

The recipients will receive the email from system mail box but Supervisor can opt/copy himself in the mail.  

5. Download full Learning history - Learner

Another impactful feature , Now User/Learner can Download All the learning History in a single PDF.

Go to Learning History > choose the Download option on the page.

 

⚠️Note :  - This feature is not applicable for Manager, Delegates and in Proxy 

                 - You have Access Reports and Run User Learning History Report permissions.

                                                                         Source: SAP

                                                

6. Enhanced Proxy Access to the New Learning Experience

Launch Proxy experience has been upgraded from legacy to New learning experience home page .The enhancement ensures that administrators experience the same interface as users, ensuring accurate troubleshooting.

                                                                                 Source: SAP

7. Advanced feature added to Custom Cards and Custom Pages in Learning Administration

Minor enhancements to the Custom Cards and Custom Pages pages to improve the administrator experience.

like : Target Group and Active period  option has been added to both custom page and custom card. And an indicator for expired custom cards/page.

                                                 Source : SAP

8. Team View on structural Hierarchy 

SAP have redesigned Team view page, Now Supervisor can view all direct & indirect reports in a tree style view.

                                                                      Source: SAP

9. Browse Library Functionality 

SAP has enhanced the Browse Library feature with the ability to browse all learning activities. Previously, the Browse Library feature only supported the ability to refine search results by category.

Browse all Learning activities is based on the below configuration :

System Administration  > Configuration > System Configuration > LIBRARY {enableBrowseLibrary}, This should be enableBrowseLibrary=True by default it was false.

                                                                               Source : SAP

Hence the upgrades were been adopted in new learning home page ,Legacy Learning Home page will be Deprecate on Nov 15, 2025.

Hope this blog helps on the updates of 1H 2025, Happy Learning 😊.

entry.title: How to migrate Boomi Integrations to SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
1. Purpose of the document

This blog provides a comprehensive guide on migrating SAP SuccessFactors HCM integrations from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS), highlighting its strategic advantages, including prepackaged integrations, Tech Envo Accelerators, Migration Framework, Migration Process,  Design considerations, and hybrid deployment options. It covers SAP’s runtime environments, deployment models, and TechEnvo’s migration accelerators, ensuring a seamless migration. With TechEnvo’s expertise and adherence to SAP best practices, organizations can modernize their integration landscape while ensuring scalability, consistency, and long-term success.

 

2. Why SAP SuccessFactors HCM customer needs to migrate from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite

  • Between 2013 and 2016, SAP SuccessFactors HCM customers used BOOMI as an integration platform to support data integration requirements in SAP SuccessFactors HCM implementation, and many customers are still running on Boomi.
  • SAP Customers have significantly invested in SAP BTP and SAP Integration Suite in the last seven years as part of S4HANA transitions and Integration Modernization (moving from SAP PO to SAP IS). 
  • Customers are migrating interfaces from Boomi to the SAP Integration Suite, aligning with the SAP Integration strategy.
  • Maintaining multiple integration platforms adds a lot of Technical Debt, and SAP customers consolidate and move to one integration platform (SAP Integration Suite) 
  • SAP Integration Suite Capabilities on par with BOOMI

3. SAP Integration Suite Capabilities and Features

SAP Integration Suite (SAP IS) is the strategic integration platform for SAP customers, offering seamless connectivity with over 3,400 prebuilt integrations and 3,800+ APIs. It provides all the capabilities of Boomi and additional advantages such as prepackaged content covering most of the SAP Solutions Integrations, AI-driven integration, and enhanced security. 

SAP Integration Suite is a comprehensive set of tools and services that enable seamless integration across various SAP and third-party systems. It helps businesses connect, automate, and optimize their processes across cloud and on-premises environments. Here are some key features and components of the SAP Integration Suite:

3.1 Key Features:

  • Connectivity: Provides pre-built connectors and adapters for integrating various SAP and third-party applications, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, and external applications like Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics.
  • Integration Flows: Facilitates the orchestration of data between applications with customizable integration flows, enabling businesses to automate workflows and synchronize data.
  • API Management: Allows businesses to manage their APIs in a secure and scalable manner, with tools to create, expose, and govern APIs across multiple environments.
  • Data Transformation: Supports the transformation of data between different formats (e.g., XML, JSON) to ensure seamless communication between systems.
  • Monitoring and Analytics: Provides dashboards and monitoring tools for real-time visibility into integration processes and system health, allowing proactive management.
  • Cloud-Based: Being cloud-native, SAP Integration Suite provides flexibility and scalability to handle evolving integration needs.
  • Security: Offers robust security features, including authentication, authorization, encryption, and secure communication for data exchanges

3.2 SAP Integration Suite Core Components:

  1. SAP Cloud Integration (CPI): A cloud-based integration platform for connecting SAP and non-SAP applications. It allows seamless integration of cloud and on-premise systems.
  2. SAP API Management: A solution to design, publish, and monitor APIs, providing tools to securely expose and consume APIs.
  3. SAP Open Connectors: A collection of pre-built connectors to a variety of third-party applications and services.
  4. SAP Integration Advisor: A tool that automates the mapping and transformation of data across systems, helping streamline the integration process.
  5. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): While the Integration Suite is a part of BTP, it works closely with other BTP services like SAP HANA, SAP AI, and SAP analytics for a comprehensive digital transformation solution.

3.3 SAP Integration Suite Use Cases:

  • ERP Integration: Connect SAP SuccessFactors HCM and SAP S/4HANA with other enterprise systems, such as CRM, Payroll, and Time, Benefits..etc, to ensure a unified view of business operations.
  • Cloud and On-Premises Integration: Link cloud-based applications (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors HCM ) with on-premises systems (e.g., SAP ECC).
  • API-First Strategy: Enable businesses to expose services as APIs and integrate them with external systems for broader connectivity.
  • Data Synchronization: Automatically sync data between systems, ensuring consistency and eliminating manual data entry errors.

3.4 SAP Integration Suite Benefits:

  • Faster Time-to-Value: Pre-built integrations and connectors reduce implementation time.
  • Scalability: Easily scale integration scenarios as your business grows and systems evolve.
  • Flexibility: The suite supports various integration scenarios, from simple point-to-point integrations to complex, multi-cloud architectures.
  • Cost Efficiency: Reduces integration complexity and costs by leveraging a unified platform for all integration needs.

In summary, SAP Integration Suite enables organizations to drive digital transformation by ensuring seamless integration across their entire IT landscape, whether using SAP or third-party applications.

 

4.Boomi to SAP Integration Suite Migration – Tech Envo Framework 

We have worked on multiple Boomi to SAP Integration Suite migration projects and developed a framework and strategy for successful migrations.

 

4.1 Scope of Boomi Integrations to be migrated to SAP Integration Suite:

This is the first step in the migration process: identifying the number of integrations currently running in the Boomi Production Atom environment and ensuring the scope is determined. 

4.2 Document pain points and current environment: 

Talk to the business and technical team supporting the integrations in the BOOMI environment and get a list of pain points and challenges. This is a critical step, and addressing these issues in the SAP Integration Suite after migration is necessary.

Common pain points : 

  1. Poor Integration design 
  2. Poor Integration coding standards 
  3. Not implementing prepackaged integrations
  4. Customizing prepackaged integrations 
  5. Heavy Groovy scripting
  6. Lack of better error handling mechanisms
  7. Lack of documentation
  8. Not using the correct SAP SuccessFactors APIs
  9. Delta Logic is missing 
  10. No encryption
  11. Authentication type issues 
  12. Tightly coupled interfaces - not easy to make updates.

4.3 Identify Integration Patterns: 

Identity integration patterns like Synchronous, Asynchronous, cloud-to-cloud, cloud-to-on-premises, On-premise—on-premise Integration, Scheduled, Real-Time, Type of Systems involved, etc., and create an SAP Integration Suite Integration patterns covering all scenarios. 

  • Synchronous Integration – Real-time data exchange with immediate response—API-to-API communication between SAP SuccessFactors and another system.


                   Fig: Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution flow of Payments canada to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Master Data Sync

  • Asynchronous Integration – Data is sent without waiting for an immediate response.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync.

Fig:  High Resolution flow of SAP SFEC to Metlife Employee Data Sync Integration.

  • Cloud-to-Cloud Integration – Data exchange between two cloud applications.Syncing data between SAP SuccessFactors and another cloud-based system(ServiceNow,Okta, Workday,SAP Concur, SAP Fieldglass, S4HANA Cloud.etc)

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

Fig: High Resolution Flowof SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to OKTA Data Sync Integration

  • Cloud-to-On-Premises Integration – Communication between a cloud-based system and an on-premises system.Triggering updates in an on-premises system from a cloud-based application(S4 HANA OP, SAP ECC, Data Lake OP, SFTP OP).

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP SFEC to ShareWorks Employee Data Sync

  • On-Premises to On-Premises Integration – Data exchange between two on-premises systems.Sending employee updates from SAP SuccessFactors to External Applications.

Fig:  Integration Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

Fig: High Resolution Flow of SAP S4HANA or SAP ECC to BankMaster Data Replication

  • Scheduled Integration – Batch processing executed at defined intervals.Daily batch transfer of employee records from SAP SuccessFactors.

Fig: Integration Flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

Fig: High Resolution flow of SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding to Qualtrics Data Sync 

  • Real-Time Integration – Event-driven or API-based real-time integration.Triggering real-time updates in one system based on events in another.
  • Employee Demographic Data Outbound Integrations

Fig: Active directory(Outbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

  • Employee Data Update Inbound Integrations

Fig: Integration Flow of Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

Fig: High Resolution Flow Active directory(Inbound) to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Demographic Data Sync

4.4 Create Migration Strategy 

  • Create a detailed strategy covering all aspects of migration:
  1. Deployment Approach and Timelines
  2. Prioritization of interfaces
  3. Technical Architecture
  4. Documenting best practices 

4.5 Map Boomi capabilities with SAP Integration Suite

Identify capabilities used in BOOMI and map which capabilities you need to use in IS. This will give a clear picture of gaps in capabilities and how they can be solved, such as connectors used, API management, Scripting, security, Retry mechanism, error logging capabilities, etc. 

Tech Envo's best practices always include using standard capabilities offered by the SAP integration suite and implementation prepacked integration before building custom integrations.  

4.6 Create Technical Architecture / Design and Development standards

Creating a technical design for each interface is critical. The technical design can be reused depending on the integration type and pattern. Create detailed technical design standards and a framework for the topics below. 

  • Development standards
  • Naming conventions
  • Reusable framework / a common framework (reuse of scripts, security materials, etc)
  • Documentation standards and deliverables
  • Interface development life cycle
  • Interface testing 
  • API best practices 

4.7 Integration Build and Testing

Ensure that the integration consultant working on the integration build has the expertise below. This will help build high-quality integrations. 

  • A clear understanding of SAP SuccessFactors HCM APIs 
  • Excellent hands-on experience in the SAP Integration suite 
  • Experience in Integration build for SAP SuccessFactors HCM
  • Able to understand BOOMI integration code (Boomi platform)

Create a detailed development process for all integrations, align on the development approach, and implement leading practices. This will ensure consultants working on the project follow the same standards and help support the interfaces better.

For example, if you are building delta logic for SAP SuccessFactors HCM data extract, ensure this logic can be reusable across interfaces. Usually, we create a reusable Groovy script. Maintaining the code would be less effort if everyone used the same approach.

4.8 Integrations Testing and comparing BOOMI/SAP Integration Suite Outputs. 

Integration testing is a critical phase of the project. Ensure you document all test cases, check if any are available from the Boomi project, and enhance the same with additional cases. Run the integration in the Boomi environment and SAP Integration suite and compare data. If data matches both platforms, logic integration in SAP integration suits and covers all requirements but follows this process for all scenarios. 

4.9 Deployment in SAP Integration Suite Production 

Once the business approves the integration, plan the deployment in the SAP Integration Suite production environment and schedule as required. Un-deploy or stop the Boomi schedule for the interfaces deployed in the SAP IS prod environment. 

5. Boomi to SAP Cloud Integration - Migration High-Level Decision Flow to determine Integration approach 

The primary goal of the below decision is to leverage prepackaged integrations as much as possible before building a custom integration solution. This approach aligns with the recommendation of SAP's leading practices and SAP clean core principles.

 

 

6. Tech Envo Migration Accelerators

TechEnvo Migration Accelerators include 74+ prepackaged integrations and templates for SAP SuccessFactors HXM in SAP Integration Suite, reusable Framework, and an advanced error-handling framework. Our team is experts in Boomi, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions, with deep knowledge of SAP SuccessFactors APIs and best practices. With 700+ integrations built using SAP Integration Suite and Boomi, we bring extensive migration experience, ensuring a seamless transition from Boomi to SAP Integration Suite. Our certified professionals have successfully executed multiple Boomi-to-SAP IS migration projects, leveraging industry-leading methodologies.

7. Tech Envo Migration Framework  – SAP SuccessFactors Integration Approach

As part of the migration, 

We develop high-quality integrations in SAP Integration Suite, utilizing its full capabilities to ensure optimal performance. Our approach prioritizes leveraging pre-delivered features with minimal customization by using standard mapping functions and predefined process steps offered by SAP Integration Suite. We also enforce industry-leading development standards aligned with SAP best practices and organizations' leading practices, ensuring consistency, maintainability, and scalability across all integrations.

 

  1. We implement direct API-based Direct integrations if the vendor has built integration and is available in the SAP App Center (e.g., Hire Right, Nakisa, JDMS, SailPoint, Omada IDM, Okta, etc.). Direct integrations avoid the middleware layer and reduce development and support costs.
  2. We implement prepackaged integrations developed by SAP, Tech Envo, or Vendor to reduce development effort and customization.
  3.  We use SAP SuccessFactors Integration Center (IC) if the Integration Center integrations require less effort to build and maintain.
  4. We have built reusable integration templates, which we can use if we need to develop a custom integration. 

 

8. SAP Integration Suite Technical Landscape, Cloud Foundry Environment

  • Cloud Foundry: The Cloud Foundry environment in SAP BTP is an open-source application runtime supporting Java, Node.js, and other languages, ideal for extending SAP products, integrating applications, and building enterprise solutions using SAP-hosted APIs. 
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/technical-landscape-cloud-foundry-environment 

 

9. Deployment Environments of SAP Integration Suite

  • Cloud Integration Runtime: A cloud-based service in SAP Integration Suite, enabling seamless data exchange between cloud and on-premise applications like Boomi Cloud  Atoms.
  • Edge Integration Cell: A hybrid integration runtime in SAP Integration Suite that allows designing in the cloud while running integrations on-premise, equivalent to Boomi On-Premise Atom
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/setting-up-and-managing-edge-integration-cell 
  • Hybrid Deployment Option: Customer can use both Cloud Integration Runtime and Edge Integration Cell - Hybrid deployment option. 

 

10. SFEC to SAP ERP/S4HANA Integrations Migration Approach

We migrate Boomi legacy SAP S4 HANA, SAP ERP HCM, SAP ECC integrations to the BIB Replication Framework for seamless. Employee and Organizational Data replication. Our expertise includes implementing BIB-based integrations for multiple customers, including SAP S/4HANA environments. We have developed accelerators to handle complex replication scenarios, such as dynamic actions, pensioners’ data replication, concurrent employment, and global assignments, ensuring a robust and efficient migration process and no impact to payroll during migration.

 

Migration Key Considerations

1.Connectivity

  • The IP address changes for  Boomi data center (Atoms) and SAP IS are different, so the firewall ports and network config must be updated with new IP range details during migration.
  • Server and Client certificates in the on-premise ERP system must also be updated during migration.
  • Any custom integrations built using SuccessFactors SMTP server for sending emails must be changed to a publicly available SMTP server, as SuccessFactors does not allow external access to its SMTP server.
  • Any locally installed atom needs to be changed to use SAP Cloud Connector (SCC)/Edge Integration. SAP Cloud Connector connects on-premise systems with SAP IS without opening tunnels.

2.Instance Strategy:

  • Depending on the license model, SAP provides two tenants (Test and Production) by default, but we recommend using a three-tier architecture.
  • Multi-tier architecture requires an additional SAP IS tenant, which may involve extra costs.

 

3.Cloud to On-Premise Connectivity

  • On-premise connections via Boomi Local Atom need migration to SAP Cloud Connector or Edge Integration Cell.
  • Proxy tools used in Boomi can still be leveraged in SAP Cloud Integration.

 

4.Interface Mapping: Existing interfaces need to be evaluated based on the following 4 buckets:

  1. Boomi Pre-Packaged to SAP IS Pre-Packaged /Direct API-based integration 
  2. Boomi custom integration to SAP IS a Pre-Packaged integration 
  3. Boomi Custom Integration to SF Integration Center Integrations
  4. Custom Boomi to Custom SAP Integration Suite 

5.Interface Prioritization

  1. Prioritize high-risk integrations such as Payroll, Time, and Benefits 
  2. Ensure SAP IS generates output with the same logic as Boomi.
  3. Validate output files between Boomi and SAP IS to streamline testing.

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Learning H1 2025 Release: Key Highlights and Features
entry.content:
Hi All,

Exciting advancements are here with the SAP SuccessFactors Learning H1 2025 release! Discover how the latest features, from AI-assisted insights to enhanced user experiences, are set to transform your learning management system. Dive into our blog to explore the key updates and see how these innovations can elevate your organization's learning and development strategies

Let's dive into the key highlights of the H1 2025 release and explore how these updates can benefit your organization.

1.AI-Assisted Image Generation in Learning

Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

AI-assisted image generation is transforming learning within SAP SuccessFactors (SF) by enhancing visual content creation and making learning materials more engaging and personalized. 

Administrators can generate custom images for course thumbnails, banners, and learning cards by simply typing a description. This feature allows for the quick creation of high-quality visuals without needing in-house design resources to modify existing items, programs, and curricula.

When you select Generate Image, a widget opens with a text box where you can enter up to 180 characters to describe the image. After entering the description, you can choose Generate to see a preview of the generated image. This process may take a minimum of 15 seconds or extend beyond two minutes, depending on the complexity of the image description.

Configuration Requirements

  • You've purchased the AI unit’s license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • Enable the AI Services Administration Assisted Image Generation setting in Admin Center.

Benefits:

  • Efficiently generate safe and relevant AI Images that can be directly applied in add/edit flows or download it for later.
  • Available for items, program, curriculum, custom cards and banners.

2.AI-Assisted Skills Inference for Programs Through Import

AI-assisted skills inference for programs through import is a powerful feature that helps organizations identify and map relevant skills from various data sources.

Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

In addition to AI-assisted skills inference for learning items, you can now bulk-associate skills with multiple learning programs through import. Use AI capabilities to extract skills from program descriptions, align the skills with TIH attribute library and associate with learning programs.

Configuration Requirements

  • You've purchased the AI unit's license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • Enable Assisted Skills Inference for Learning in the Learning section of the AI Service Administration page.

Benefits:

  • Skills interface for programs in bulk
  • Enables customers to use skills they’ve configured in Talent Intelligence Hub within Learning
  • Enhance admin productivity by removing the need to manually identify skills

3.Auto-Update Attribute Ratings for Open Content Network Items Using a property

This feature allows for the automatic updating of attribute ratings for Open Content Network (OCN) items using a specified property. This ensures that the ratings are always current and accurately reflect the quality and relevance of the content

Enablement: Automatically On

Configuration Requirements

  • Enable the auto-update feature in the Learning Administration settings.
  • Specify the property that will be used to update the attribute ratings automatically

Benefits:

  • Empowers administrators to effectively maintain the configuration in bulk
  • Ensures that completing an OCN item automatically updates its associated attributes in Growth Portfolio                

4.Automatic Process for Verified Email Domain Synchronization

Enablement: Automatically on

This feature automates the synchronization of verified email domains between the SAP SuccessFactors Platform and the Learning Management System (LMS). This ensures that email communications are sent from trusted and verified domains, reducing the risk of delivery issues

Configuration Requirements:

1.You have the Enable Email Domain Validation enabled from Learning Administration >System      Administration >Configuration >Global Application Settings >Mail

2.Navigate to Learning Administration:

Go to System Administration → Automatic Processes → Verified Email Domain Synchronization

3.Schedule the Process:

  • Select the Schedule this Process checkbox.
  • Set the frequency (daily, weekly, etc.) and specify the time of day for the synchronization

Benefit:

  • This feature enables you to monitor synchronization jobs and identify issues with email domain synchronization between SAP SuccessFactors Platform and Learning.

5. Changes to Permission for Overdue Training Reminders

Enablement: Automatically On

The H1 2025 release includes updates to the permissions for overdue training reminders. These changes aim to improve the management and customization of reminder notifications for overdue training items

To send overdue training reminders from the Team View page or the new Learning home page, you need the new permission, Manager Reminders. SAP added this for all users who are currently managers and have the Access Team's Learning and Access Direct Report permissions.

Benefits:

  • Allows administrators to control which users can trigger reminders
  • Streamline manager notifications with Manager Reminders Notifications
  • Ensure manager can remind overdue subordinates to finish their pending assignment

6.Configuration to Support Unload Browser Events for Online Content

Enablement: Automatically on

This feature allows the LMS to support unload browser events, which can help track when users navigate away from online content. This ensures that learning progress is accurately recorded and helps prevent data loss

SAP delivered the enableContentUnloadEventUsage configuration. This configuration extends temporary support for unload browser events beyond the initial vendor deprecation date.

Configuration Requirements

The enableContentUnloadEventUsage configuration is enabled by default (System Administration >Configuration> System Configuration >LMS_ADMIN). This setting will be removed in a future release to align with Google’s plan to deprecate support across all websites in 2025. 

7.Download Learning History to PDF File

Enablement: Automatically On

This feature allows users to download their learning history as a PDF file. This provides a convenient way to keep a record of completed courses and training programs

As a learner, you can now use the Learning History page to download all your completed learning activities in a PDF file.

Configuration requirements:

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

  • You have Access Reports and Run User Learning History Report permissions.
  • You have the Access Learning History permission.

Benefits:

  • Streamlines the export process by providing direct access to all of user’s learning history in a PDF format
  • Saves time by eliminating the need to generate separate reports

8.Editable Overdue Training Reminders

Enablement: Automatically on

Manager can preview and edit the email messages before sending

Configuration Requirements:

The new Learning home page is enabled.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

To send overdue training reminders from the Team View page you need the new permission, Manager Reminders. You can access the permission from System Administration >Security> Role Management.

Benefits

  • This feature to give our customers more control over their overdue training reminders
  • Allow managers to preview and edit individual reminders content before sending
  • Provide managers with the option to customize subject lines and message body

9.Enhanced Proxy Access to the New Learning Experience

Enablement: Automatically On

This feature enhances proxy access, allowing designated proxies to access the new learning experience on behalf of other users. This ensures that proxies can manage learning activities, view progress, and assist with training needs more effectively

In the previous version, the Launch Proxy action redirected you to the legacy Learning home page, even if the user had the new Learning experience enabled.

Configuration Requirements

The new Learning home page is enabled.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

You have the Administrator Proxy for Learning permission to launch a proxy session.

Benefits:

  • Admins can troubleshoot issues by proxying to Learner’s new learning Homepage
  • Admin gets information when they are proxy mode and can end proxy mode and land on the user summary page

10. Enhancements to Custom Cards and Custom Pages in Learning Administration

Enablement: Automatically On

The H1 2025 release includes enhancements to custom cards and custom pages in Learning Administration. These updates provide more flexibility and personalization options for administrators to create engaging and informative content

You can filter custom cards by Target Group and Active Period.

An indicator is displayed for expired custom cards to indicate that the cards are inactive and aren't visible to the target users. The date range of an expired card is highlighted, indicating that the active period has ended.

When choosing an image for custom cards (in the Card wizard), the guidelines now include the recommended dimensions of 1024 x 1024 pixels information to help you maintain optimal image resolution

Enhancements on custom pages:

On a custom page, you can filter banners in the Banners section and custom cards in the Sections section by Target Group and Active Period

On a custom page, an indicator is displayed for expired banners and custom cards in their respective sections to indicate that they're inactive and aren't visible to the target users. The date range of an expired banner and card is highlighted, indicating that its active period has ended.

Configuration Requirements

The new Learning home page is enabled.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

You have the Manage Custom Experience permission.

11.Improved Browse Library Functionality

Enablement: Customer configured

SAP enhanced the Browse Library feature with the ability to browse all learning activities.

Instantly browse all learning available without any Key word

Configuration Requirements:

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites

Users need the Library >Access Advanced Library Search permission in their role. You can access the permission from System Administration >Security >Role Management.

Benefits:

  • Enable users to access their Learning library
  • Allow learners to discover all learning without need to enter any keyword
  • Admin can decide to show both browse by category and browse all or only browse all
  • Refine search results with search filters like duration skill, and flagged after selecting browse all

12.Manager Permission to Remove Items in Team View Now Option on User Settings Page

Enablement: Automatically On

The H1 2025 release introduces a new option on the User Settings page that allows managers to remove items from the Team View. This enhancement provides managers with greater control over the learning activities of their team members

Configuration Requirements:

1.System Administration >Security> Role Management > Manage Assignments

2.Navigate to Learning Administration >System Administration> Application Administration >User Settings add three selected options for these settings

  • Items assigned by an Administrator
  • Curricula assigned by an Administrator
  • Programs assigned by an Administrator

Benefits:

  • Empower managers to cleanup duplicate outdated trainings
  • Enable managers to remove manager assignments and employee self-assignments using Manage Assignments workflow (legacy behavior)
  • Allow managers to remove admin assigned learning entities based on configuration.

13.Near Real Time Updates to Learning Entities in Enhanced Search

Enablement: Automatically On

Changes made to learning activity entities and reference entities are now reflected in the enhanced search results in near real time.

Most changes are reflected within 30 minutes, though many updates occur within just a few minutes. Longer update times can be expected in some scenarios, such as when updating curricula thumbnails or making bulk changes that affect more than ten entities. The duration of an update also depends on the number of changes already in progress.

Benefits:

  • Helps ensure that learners have access to the most current learning content and filter options when searching their libraries

Note: To allow time for testing, this enhancement will have a staggered release to Preview and Production, based on Data Center

14.New Team View Reporting Hierarchy

Enablement: Automatically on

Simplified and clean experience for manager to navigating through team hierarchy

SAP added the ability for managers to see team hierarchies on the Team View page. SAP replaced the flat team view where all direct and indirect reports were listed on one level with the updated view placing a selectable icon that expands the hierarchy to show direct reports.

Previously the list of team members was organized in a flat structure showing all direct and indirect reports

Configuration Requirements

  • The new Learning home page is enabled.
  • The System Administration >Configuration >System Configuration >LMS_ADMIN >reportingLevelsToDisplay configuration setting must be set as follows:
    • If set to 0, the user can see all direct and indirect subordinates. This is the default value.
    • If set to 1, the user can see their direct reports only, which are one level down the reporting hierarchy.
    • If set to 2 or more, the user can see that number of levels down the reporting hierarchy.

Benefits:

  • Establish clear distinction between direct and indirect employees
  • Monitor learning progress of entire team
  • Effectively manage team assignments and resolve learning blockers

15.Option to Use Application Notification Email for Overdue Training Reminders

SAP added the option to use the Application Notification User Email for manager email notifications. When this option is selected, overdue training reminders will not be sent to the recipient using the manager's email but will use the Application Notification User Email instead.

Enablement: Automatically on

Configuration Requirements

The Application Notification User Email is not blank and Enable email is Enabled.

Benefits:

  • This feature to make sure our customer's email delivery procedures for email notifications are configurable to their standards.

16 Optional Curricula Assignments Available in the New Learning Experience

As learners, you can access optional assignments and classes from your assigned curricula and sub-curricula directly from the new Learning home page.

Enablement- Automatically on

Configuration Requirements

  • The new Learning home page is enabled.
  • You've created an assignment type as not required on the Assignment Types page and applied it for the content of a curriculum from Curricula >Content >Manage Content> Assignment Type.

17.Optional Surveys Available in the New Learning Experience

Enablement: Automatically On

As learners, you can now launch optional surveys directly from the new Learning home page to provide feedback on your completed training. Your feedback helps enhance and improve the learning content.

As administrators, you can now track the number of removed surveys in the Removed column, along with assigned and completed surveys, when reviewing survey statistics from Content >Survey> Item Usage.

Configuration Requirements

  • The new Learning home page is enabled.
  • You have an item where:
    • a course feedback survey is assigned with the Required for Item Completion check box disabled.
    • a follow-up survey is assigned.

Benefits:

  • Optional surveys are now available in the new learning experience (Course feedback +Follow-up)
  • Learners can remove surveys (managers can remove follow-up surveys) depending on the survey configuration
  • Survey notification now directly takes the learners to the specific survey
  • Statics for “Removed” surveys now maintained under surveys> Item usage

18.Use the AI Services Administration Page to Enable AI-Assisted Skill Features for Learning

Enablement:Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

You can now use the AI Services Administration page to enable AI-assisted skill features, including Assisted Skills Inference for Learning and Assisted Skills Association with Open Content Network Items.

Configuration Requirements

  • You've purchased the AI unit’s license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • Ensure the integration between Learning and SAP SuccessFactors Platform is sealed.
  • Ensure that you've upgraded to Talent Intelligence Hub and the integration between Learning and Talent Intelligence Hub is completed.
  • Ensure that you have your SAP SuccessFactors Platform and Learning available within the same tenant.

Benefits:

  • Allows you manage all AI-assisted skills features from single centralized page in Admin Centre

Important Links:

H1 2025 Release Updates

https://www.sap.com/documents/2025/04/2e222a5c-ff7e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html 

Key Highlights from the 1H 2025 Release of Success... - SAP Community

Product Release and Road Map Information

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/successfactors/product-release-road-map 

If you found this blog helpful and insightful, we encourage you to share it with your colleagues, friends, and professional network

entry.title: Smarter HR with Joule: What's New in SAP SuccessFactors – 1H 2025 Release
entry.content:
SAP’s AI copilot, Joule, has had a major upgrade in the 1H 2025 release across SAP SuccessFactors modules, including Employee Central, Recruiting, Learning, and Onboarding. Here’s a concise summary of what’s new and real screenshots from SAP documentation.

1. Joule Now Available on Mobile

SAP has officially rolled out Joule in the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app. Users can perform HR tasks like requesting time off or updating contact info using conversational prompts directly on their phones.

 

2. New Use Cases for Position Management (March 2025)

Two new transactional use cases are now supported:

  • Change "To Be Hired" Status of Position
  • Change Working Time for Position

3. Full Upgrade to New Conversational Experience (January 2025)

All Joule use cases now follow a simplified, intuitive chat-based interface:

  • New: Create Time Off (Transactional)
  • Upgraded: Create Positions, Edit Contact Info, Job Changes, Promotions, Transfers & more

4. Enhancements to Employee Central Quick Actions

  • Attachments now supported for key events (e.g., Promotion, Location Change)
  • New templates for One-Time Payment and Marital Status Change
  • Phone number formatting improvements

 

5. Expanded Learning & Performance Use Cases

Users can now:

  • Approve training requests (internal, external, with/without e-sign)
  • Manage and edit performance goals, add comments, assign buddies
  • View and comment on development goals

6. Improved Recruiting, Platform, and Succession Integration

Joule now supports:

  • Approving job requisitions and offers
  • Viewing job profiles, mentoring requests, and succession nominations

7. Config & Permission Requirements

To activate new features:

  • Joule must be enabled in the backend
  • Users must have “Access to Joule” permission under General User Permissions
  • Templates and Data Models must be updated for attachments

Summary

The 1H 2025 release of Joule marks a huge leap in conversational AI-driven HR transformation. From mobile access and deeper integration across modules to intuitive self-services and actionable insights, Joule empowers both employees and managers like never before.

#SAPJoule #SAPSuccessFactors #JouleUseCases #HXMExperience #DigitalHR #AIinHR #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Vendor Consignment work around in SAP BYD
entry.content:
Hello SAP ByD experts,

We're implementing vendor consignment inventory (where suppliers stock goods at our site, billed upon consumption) in SAP Business ByDesign. While we understand ByD doesn't have native vendor consignment processing like S/4HANA, i'd appreciate suggestions on work arounds

entry.title: Re: How to track pallets across goods movements, deliveri...
entry.content:
Hello @s0011646320 

Pallets are tracked with Handling Unit Management functionality. However, notice that in general case it is not possible to track a pallet through the entire supply chain i.e. from goods receipt, through production till outbound delivery as a pallet/handling unit is a transient object - a raw material is received on a pallet/handling unit, then it is moved to production, once the raw material on the pallet is fully consumed, the pallet is gone. Even if the product is received on another pallet, there is no functionality to tell that a given pallet of a product was produced from a given pallet of a raw material. 

If you need this kind of tracking, you need to use Batch Management 

and Batch Where-Used List which is available in Batch Information Cockpit

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski
